chemistry.scope_summary: The paper demonstrates the photooxidation of furfural to hydroxybutenolide using methylene blue as photosensitizer. Also shows the same reaction can be performed with Ru(bpy)3Cl2 under blue light irradiation.

optimization_table.key_findings: Optimal conditions achieved with 0.4 M furfural, 4 mol% catalyst loading, 0.5 mL/min flow rate, 1 bar oxygen pressure

figures.flow_path: segmented flow and coaxial flow configurations
figures.key_information: Comparison of three flow synthesis approaches for hydroxy butenolide and myrtenol production with yields of 98%, 88%, and >99% respectively. Daily outputs ranging from 3.6 g/day to 26 g/day with weekly output >200 g for approach C.
figures.caption: Fig. 1 A. Our segmented flow design for the photooxidation of furfural. B. Previously described coaxial flow design for the photooxidation of β-pinene; with liquid phase on outside. C. Coaxial flow photooxidation of furfural using the modular photoreactor OctoColor; with liquid phase on inside for convenience.

figures.flow_path: IN → coiled reactor with light illumination → OUT → BPR
figures.light_source_position: external illumination from LED array above coiled reactor
figures.key_information: Multi-component photoreactor setup with LED illumination array, cooling system, and pressure regulation
figures.caption: Fig. 2 A. Schematic exploded view of OctoColor reactor. B. Schematic exploded view of LED plate. For details of construction, see SI OctoColor construction manual. C. Schematic representation of a coaxial flow reactor.

figures.flow_path: oxygen and methylene blue merge into coaxial flow section, then through illuminated reactor coil to product
figures.key_information: Photochemical oxidation of furfural using methylene blue (0.1-4 mol%) as photocatalyst, OctoColor light source at 635 nm, residence time 2-16 min at room temperature

figures.flow_path: coaxial flow through reactor with light irradiation
figures.key_information: Photochemical oxidation of furfural using Ru(bpy)3Cl2 catalyst (4 mol%) in MeOH (0.4 M) under O2 (1 bar) and 455 nm LED irradiation at room temperature, achieving >99% yield of hydroxy butenolide
figures.caption: Fig. 3 Photooxidation of furfural towards hydroxybutenolide using Ru(bpy)3Cl2 as photosensitizer. OctoColor arranged with blue LED plates. Reaction conditions in coaxial semi-permeable flow: 0.4 M furfural in methanol, Ru(bpy)3Cl2 (4 mol%), rt, oxygen, 455 nm, 8.1 mL internal volume.

translation_logic.batch_limitation: No batch baseline provided for direct comparison
translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous production with high yield (>99%), scalable throughput (29 g/day), and improved reactor design allowing 8-fold improvement over segmented flow
translation_logic.what_changed: Reactor design from segmented to coaxial flow using modular OctoColor photoreactor
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Photooxidation reaction chemistry with methylene blue photosensitizer
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Continuous flow operation with pressure regulation via BPR

figure_descriptions: Comparison of three flow synthesis approaches showing reactor configurations and performance metrics
Detailed schematic of OctoColor reactor construction with LED illumination system
Coaxial flow reactor setup for photooxidation with methylene blue
Alternative setup using Ru(bpy)3Cl2 photosensitizer with blue LED irradiation

conflicts.figure_value: BPR shown in Fig 2 and Fig 3 last panel
conflicts.resolution: BPR is present based on figure evidence

notes: High confidence extraction with good quantitative data from figures. No batch baseline limits comparison analysis. Catalyst loading shows conflict between 0.5 mol% (pass 2) and 4 mol% (optimization table) - the 4 mol% appears to be the optimized condition.
